quizzes.title: Applied Microbial Remediation: Mould Fundamentals — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: Indoors, temperature and a food source are almost always available to mould. Which factor do we therefore control to prevent mould establishing?
options: Light levels
Moisture
Air pressure
The age of the building

questions.questionText: Roughly how quickly can mould growth begin on a material that stays wet?
options: Within one to two days
Only after several months
Only after a full year of dampness
Mould never grows on building materials indoors

questions.questionText: In the Condition 1/2/3 framing, what does Condition 1 describe, and what is its significance to a remediation?
options: Actual mould growth; it is the starting point of every job
Settled spores dispersed from a growth area; it is the most contaminated state
A normal fungal ecology with no actual growth; it is the target end state of remediation
A sealed containment zone under negative pressure

questions.questionText: An area is 'primarily contaminated with settled spores dispersed directly or indirectly from a Condition 3 area'. Which condition is this?
options: Condition 1
Condition 2
Condition 3
Condition 4

questions.questionText: Why is finding and fixing the moisture source treated as part of the remediation rather than a separate task?
options: Because insurers will not pay unless the leak is photographed
Because remediation will fail and mould will return if the water that caused it remains active
Because the moisture source determines the colour-coding of equipment
It is not part of remediation; plumbing is always a separate trade's concern

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of holding a mould containment under negative pressure?
options: To make the air movers work harder inside the zone
So air flows into the work area through any gaps rather than out of it, preventing contamination drifting into clean spaces
To raise the temperature inside the containment
To remove the need for HEPA air filtration

questions.questionText: In the remediation sequence, when should containment and engineering controls be established?
options: After the contaminated materials have already been removed
Before disturbing any contaminated material
Only if an occupational hygienist requests it
At the very end, just before verification

questions.questionText: While mould is still present, which equipment is used for drying, and which is withheld?
options: Air movers only; dehumidifiers are withheld
Air filtration devices (AFDs/air scrubbers) and dehumidifiers only; air movers are withheld until after remediation and a clearance
All drying equipment including air movers, run at maximum
No drying equipment of any kind may be used at any stage

questions.questionText: Why must air movers NOT be run while mould is present?
options: They use too much power on a 10 A circuit
Their high-velocity surface airflow aerosolises spores and spreads contamination through the containment and building
They cannot be tested and tagged
They lower the humidity too quickly for mould to be removed

questions.questionText: When is a mould remediation considered finished?
options: As soon as the visible mould has been wiped away
When the area is verified as returned to Condition 1 — visibly clean, dried to the dry standard, and cleared (with independent clearance where warranted) before containment is removed
When the client says the smell has gone
When the invoice has been issued in AUD
